text.title: Headroom Cost Model Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Resource scaled up for a spike that lasted 5 minutes; bill 30% higher.
antipattern.reason: Utilisation measured as instantaneous; spike triggered over-provision.
antipattern.note: Fix: Switch to p95-30d. Re-evaluate provisioned capacity against the smoothed metric.

antipattern.description: NAT-GW saturated; service degrades; capacity planning missed it.
antipattern.note: Fix: Audit inventory completeness. Add network resources + NAT-GW + LB. Backfill metrics.

antipattern.description: Cost rose 20% but team can't tell which resource drove it.
antipattern.note: Fix: Capture cost-per-unit per resource. Recompute monthly cost from units × unit cost. Drives optimisation correctly.

antipattern.description: Q4 traffic spike; capacity insufficient; degraded service for 2 weeks.
antipattern.note: Fix: Build quarterly projection from product roadmap. Pre-provision before the spike.

antipattern.description: Cost rose 50% in June; investigation started in September; root cause forgotten.
antipattern.note: Fix: Auto-open ticket on >30% deviation. Engineering leader pinged. Investigation closed within 1 business day.
